seoTitle: Mount Mary vs Mount Mcintyre — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Mary and Mount Mcintyre. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Mary vs Mount Mcintyre: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Mary and Mount Mcintyre.
heading: Mount Mary vs Mount Mcintyre — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Mary, South Australia and Mount Mcintyre, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Mcintyre has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mount Mary (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Mary or Mount Mcintyre safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Mary has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Mount Mcintyre scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Mary scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Mcintyre scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Mary or Mount Mcintyre?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Mcintyre has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
